channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🌏 Hollow Deals, Tricky Negotiations, and State Visits | War on the Rocks
item.description: Welcome to The Adversarial. Every other week, we’ll provide you with expert analysis on America’s greatest challengers: China, Russia, Iran, North Korea, and jihadists. Read more below.***IranThere has been increasing chatter that Washington and Tehran are nearing an agreement…

item.title: ⚛️ BAE Systems wins Army’s Soft Kill APS award, with first phase valued at $20 million | Breaking Defense
item.description: The system uses BAE’s Rapid Optical Observation and Kill (ROOK) program, which takes down threats by “confusing,” or jamming missile systems or drones.

item.title: 🏛️ Army Wants More Sensor-Laden Surveillance Balloons Over The Pacific | The War Zone
item.description: High-altitude balloons are an increasingly critical part of the Army's future combat strategy, from providing networking to sensing to delivering kinetic effects. The post Army Wants More Sensor-Laden Surveillance Balloons Over The Pacific appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ US Army turns resupply drone into rocket launcher in new test | Defense News
item.description: Though the drone was designed to haul supplies across the battlefield, the Army recently tried turning it into a rocket launcher.

item.title: 🔐 Severity Of America’s Depleted Advanced Weapons Stockpiles Detailed In New Report | The War Zone
item.description: America's stockpile of critical standoff and air and missile defense weapons is now a strategic vulnerability foes could take advantage of. The post Severity Of America’s Depleted Advanced Weapons Stockpiles Detailed In New Report appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 The Iran War Has Only Worsened Sudan’s Conflict | Foreign Policy
item.description: The country is uniquely vulnerable to regional shocks.

item.title: 📍 Turkey’s defense industry is leaning into autonomy and targeting Gulf markets | Breaking Defense
item.description: Middle East Bureau Chief Agnes Helou shares what she learned at Turkey’s SAHA defense expo.

item.title: 🏛️ The Navy used drones to sink a retired warship | Defense One
item.description: Lessons from the SINKEX are shaping the service’s plans to buy and fight.

item.title: 📍 Erdogan Is Forcibly Designing His Own Opposition | Foreign Policy
item.description: Turkey is moving from repressing the opposition to reshaping it.

item.title: 🏴 Ukrainian Gripen Fighters To Arrive In 2027, Long-Range Meteor Missiles Claimed To Be Included | The War Zone
item.description: Sweden’s transfer of Gripens will give Ukraine more than a new fighter, pairing its trademark dispersed operations with Meteor missiles and radar planes. The post Ukrainian Gripen Fighters To Arrive In 2027, Long-Range Meteor Missiles Claimed To Be Included appeared first on The…

item.title: 📍 Cubans Abandon the American Dream | Foreign Policy
item.description: As conditions on the island worsen, people are leaving—just not for the United States.

item.title: 🔐 In cyber race against China, CYBERCOM bets on ‘quality over quantity’ | Breaking Defense
item.description: Rep. Don Bacon sounded the alarm to Breaking Defense about China’s purported 10:1 workforce advantage when it comes to “offensive” cyber operations.

item.title: 🏴 What Would Relations with Post-War Russia Look Like? | War on the Rocks
item.description: Rose Gottemoeller joined Ryan in Washington. They discussed how the West might think about relations with Russia once the war with Ukraine ends, as well as nuclear diplomacy and other critical issues. Gottemoeller was the deputy secretary general of NATO and, before that, served…

item.title: 🔐 Information fires: Building C-C5ISRT advantage in competition | Atlantic Council
item.description: Countering adversary command, control, communications, computers, cyber, intelligence, surveillance, reconnaissance, and targeting (C5ISRT) has emerged as a critical operational priority. The post Information fires: Building C-C5ISRT advantage in competition appeared first on…

item.title: 📍 Lithuania to buy Patria armored vehicles, eyes local industry share | Defense News
item.description: A contract is expected to be signed with the Finnish producer in 2027.

item.title: 📍 Promote the antidote: Reducing the risk from toxins | Atlantic Council
item.description: The policymaking community is not fully aware of the expansion of the threat from toxins. In this report, the Bipartisan Commission on Biodefense at Atlantic Council outlines the threat and how to negate it. The post Promote the antidote: Reducing the risk from toxins appeared…

item.title: 🛡️ How terrorism imperils Pakistan’s polio eradication efforts | Atlantic Council
item.description: Pakistan has never been closer to ending polio, but frontline health workers are operating inside one of the world's most violent security environments. The post How terrorism imperils Pakistan’s polio eradication efforts appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 Bibi’s Manichean Politics | Foreign Policy
item.description: Over three decades, Netanyahu’s pivot to divisive electioneering has paid off.

item.title: 📍 Austal USA names new president | Breaking Defense
item.description: Austal USA announced in February that Gene Miller was serving as interim president.

item.title: 🏴 Ukraine to buy 20 new Gripen jets, Sweden to donate older jets sooner | Defense News
item.description: “We need these jets, and for us this is really a new page for Ukraine,” Zelenskyy said.

item.title: 🏴 After delivery of first tanks to Norway, EuroTrophy looks to wider European future | Breaking Defense
item.description: “This procurement of Leopard tanks to Norway came at the right timing with the rebuilding of German armed forces and their tank fleet and we could join with KNDS and [the] German and Norwegian government[s] to create the new baseline of the Leopard,” EuroTrophy Managing Director…

item.title: 📍 The Madman Strikes Back | Foreign Policy
item.description: Nixon’s theory on the power of unpredictability didn’t account for an actual madman adversary.

item.title: 🏴 Friedrich Merz Doesn’t Understand the EU | Foreign Policy
item.description: Germany’s chancellor keeps complaining about the source of the continent’s power.

item.title: 🏴 Ukraine’s battlefield success should not lead us to underestimate Russia | Atlantic Council
item.description: The Russian invasion of Ukraine has failed to achieve any of its key goals, but this reflects Ukrainian strength rather than Russian weakness. Putin's army remains a formidable threat to Europe that must not be underestimated, writes Peter Dickinson. The post Ukraine’s…

item.title: 🌍 Trump Should Just Admit He Screwed Up | Foreign Policy
item.description: The Iran war was obviously a mistake. Why not say so?

item.title: 🌏 Building a State-Controlled Knowledge Infrastructure: The Evolution of Distance Education in North Korea | 38 North
item.description: More often than not, the outside world dismisses North Korean digital education as ... The post Building a State-Controlled Knowledge Infrastructure: The Evolution of Distance Education in North Korea appeared first on 38 North.

item.title: 🔐 The Weaponization of GLOMAG: How Rivals Co-opt U.S. Sanctions to Target Business and Political Opponents | Just Security
item.description: The U.S. human rights and anticorruption sanctions architecture is vulnerable to exploitation by the very actors it was designed to confront. The post The Weaponization of GLOMAG: How Rivals Co-opt U.S. Sanctions to Target Business and Political Opponents appeared first on Just…

item.title: 🌏 The Historic U.S. Defense Budget Request Needs a Sound Indo-Pacific Policy | Just Security
item.description: The Trump administration's proposed $1.45 trillion defense budget for fiscal 2027 comes up short in three key ways for U.S. security in the Indo-Pacific. The post The Historic U.S. Defense Budget Request Needs a Sound Indo-Pacific Policy appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🌍 Follow the Law, not the Plan: Legal Considerations for Third States in Gaza | Just Security
item.description: Third State’s support for Trump’s Gaza plan must remain strictly conditioned on compliance with international law and be continuously reassessed in light of evolving facts. The post Follow the Law, not the Plan: Legal Considerations for Third States in Gaza appeared first on…

item.title: 🏛️ Germany’s defense minister makes rare personal pitch for submarine deal in Ottawa | Defense News
item.description: “Canada opting for the 212CD would mean to consistently and sustainably pursue the transatlantic path towards closer integration of our economies.”
